revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大東宮]]


{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
|name = Daitō-gū
|image = File:Daitougu.jpg
|location = {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|en|Okinawa Prefecture|qid=Q766445|lt=Okinawa Prefecture|1=Okinawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Shimajiri District, Okinawa|en|Shimajiri District, Okinawa|qid=Q1206950|lt=Shimajiri District, Okinawa|1=Shimajiri District, Okinawa}}, {{ill|Kitadaitō, Okinawa|en|Kitadaitō, Okinawa|qid=Q1207554|lt=Kitadaitō|1=Kitadaitō, Okinawa}}, Aza Nakano
|coordinates ={{Coord|25.944|N|131.302667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} 
|deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}
|shrine_Rank = 
|founded = Meiji 36 (1903)
|other_names = 
|annual_festival = September 22 (Shinkō Festival), September 23 (Annual Festival)
|map = Japan Okinawa
|location = {{ill|Kitadaito|qid=Q1207554|1=Kitadaitō, Okinawa}}
}}
'''Daitō-gū''' (大東宮, ''Daitō-gū'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located on {{ill|Kitadaitōjima|en|Kitadaitōjima|qid=Q1111504|lt=Kitadaitō|1=Kitadaitōjima}}, {{ill|Shimajiri District, Okinawa|en|Shimajiri District, Okinawa|qid=Q1206950|lt=Shimajiri District, Okinawa|1=Shimajiri District, Okinawa}}, {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|en|Okinawa Prefecture|qid=Q766445|lt=Okinawa Prefecture|1=Okinawa Prefecture}}, Japan. The shrine's forest serves as a habitat for the {{ill|Daitō Megabat|ja|ダイトウオオコウモリ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daitō Megabat|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クビワオオコウモリ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クビワオオコウモリ|1=Ryukyu flying fox|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q575969}}, a '''National Natural Monument''', making it an excellent site for nocturnal wildlife observation.
== deity ==
Like {{ill|Daitō Shrine (Minamidaitō)|ja|大東神社 (南大東村)|1=Daitō Shrine (Minamidaito Village)|lt=Daitō Shrine|kk= (Minamidaito Village)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daitō Shrine (Minamidaitō)|13=User:Immanuelle/Daitō Shrine (Minamidaito Village)|qq=}} in {{ill|Minamidaitō, Okinawa|en|Minamidaitō, Okinawa|qid=Q1351200|lt=Minamidaitō|1=Minamidaitō, Okinawa}}, the shrine enshrines {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}.
== History ==
In Meiji 36 (1903), following the establishment of the settlement on {{ill|Minamidaitōjima|en|Minamidaitōjima|qid=Q1204188|lt=Minamidaitō|1=Minamidaitōjima}} in Meiji 33 (1900), the {{ill|Daitō Islands|en|Daitō Islands|qid=Q697792|lt=Daitō Islands|1=Daitō Islands}} development group led by {{ill|Hanzaemon Tamaki|ja|玉置半右衛門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanzaemon Tamaki|1=Tamaoki Han'emon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11572749}} initiated the settlement of {{ill|Kitadaitōjima|en|Kitadaitōjima|qid=Q1111504|lt=Kitadaitō|1=Kitadaitōjima}}. During this process, the shrine was founded by '''Tamaki Shōkai''', a company engaged in sugar production. Initially, the shrine was located on '''Kogane-yama''' (Golden Mountain), in the western part of the island (now the site of the {{ill|Kitadaitō Lighthouse|ja|北大東灯台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitadaitō Lighthouse|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), but was later moved to its current location in the island's central area.
== Festivals ==
* '''Annual Festival (September 23)''' – Similar to the festival at '''Daitō Shrine (Minamidaitō)''', '''Daitō-gū’s annual festival''' takes place from '''September 22 to 23'''. The festival features a '''procession of mikoshi (portable shrines)''' throughout the island. Within the shrine grounds, '''Okinawan sumo''' (沖縄角力) and '''Edo-period sumo''' (江戸相撲) are performed as ritual offerings, reflecting a unique blend of traditions from settlers from {{ill|Hachijō-jima|en|Hachijō-jima|qid=Q1191120|lt=Hachijō Island|1=Hachijō-jima}} and immigrants from Okinawa.
== Related Shrines ==
* {{ill|Shinmei shrines|en|Shinmei shrines|qid=Q11590310|lt=Shimmei Shrine|1=Shinmei shrines}}
* '''Other shrines in Kitadaitō''' – In addition to Daitō-gū, the following shrines exist in '''Kitadaitō''':
** {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kitadaitō)|ja|金刀比羅宮 (北大東村)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Kitadaitō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
** {{ill|Ebisu Shrine (Kitadaitō)|ja|恵比寿宮 (北大東村)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu Shrine (Kitadaitō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
** {{ill|Akiha Shrine (Kitadaitō)|ja|秋葉神社 (北大東村)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiha Shrine (Kitadaitō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
